name: Achievement System
description: A persistent milestone system that records notable actions, grants recognition, and may unlock rewards.
design_notes: Achievement systems are progress records, not only popups. Useful achievements name the tracked event, completion threshold, reward policy, hidden status, and account_progression_state boundary so players can understand what counts and developers can avoid duplicate grants.
design_purpose: Recognize optional mastery, exploration, collection, or challenge completion.
Expose long-term goals that sit alongside the main progression path.
Trigger cosmetic, currency, or meta-progression rewards from durable milestones.
player_fantasy: The player feels that unusual accomplishments and long-term mastery are noticed by the game.

parameters.name: achievement_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier used for tracking, saves, platform mapping, and reward grants.

parameters.name: tracked_event_id
parameters.description: Event source that increments or completes the achievement.

parameters.name: completion_threshold
parameters.description: Count or score required before the achievement becomes complete.

parameters.name: reward_grant_id
parameters.description: Optional reward transaction issued once when completion is committed.

parameters.name: retroactive_scan_rule
parameters.description: Rule for awarding achievements from existing save data after content changes.

edge_cases: An achievement is added after players already satisfied its requirement and needs retroactive unlock logic.
A tracked_event_id fires multiple times during replay, reconnect, or save migration.
Secret achievements need progress tracking without revealing hidden text prematurely.
Platform services can fail after the local achievement has already committed.
common_bugs: Achievement completion is stored locally but the reward_grant_id runs every login.
Progress counters reset during save migration because achievement_id changed.
UI listens to predicted events and shows completion for actions later rejected by the server.
Platform achievement calls are treated as authoritative and overwrite the game's own account_progression_state.
balancing_notes: Achievement rewards should not force every player into obscure challenge behavior unless they are optional cosmetics or small bonuses.
Use a mix of short, medium, and long thresholds so the system has visible progress without becoming checklist noise.
Retroactive unlocks reduce frustration after patches but should still validate that old data is trustworthy.
accessibility_notes: Provide text descriptions, progress counters, and a way to review missed notifications.
Avoid requiring precision-only or sensory-only challenges for mandatory progression rewards without alternate routes.

implementation_notes.unity: Route gameplay events through an achievement service keyed by tracked_event_id; commit achievement_id completion and reward_grant_id once, then mirror to platform SDKs after local state is durable.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep achievement definitions in Resources or JSON and store account_progression_state separately from UI nodes; run retroactive_scan_rule during controlled save migration only.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a subsystem or PlayerState component to aggregate tracked events, validate server-side completions for networked games, and call platform achievement APIs after internal persistence succeeds.
implementation_notes.web: Use an event reducer that updates achievement progress and completion transaction IDs; persist before showing unlock UI so refreshes cannot duplicate reward grants.

relationships.reason: Achievements and daily challenges can share account progress counters, claim state, and platform service sync.
Achievements can reinforce long-term goals without directly changing run balance.
Platform achievement services may mirror internal unlock state for profile-level rewards.

example_games.title: Steam achievements
example_games.note: Provide platform-level achievement records for many PC games.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Uses in-game prophecies as structured long-term achievement-like goals.
